message.content: Search the web for the correct GitHub repository URL for the "nanobanana" extension for the Google Gemini CLI. The Gemini CLI uses extensions installed via `gemini extensions install <github-url>`. Find the actual, working GitHub repo for nanobanana. Also check if there's an official Gemini CLI extensions marketplace or registry. Return the correct install URL.

Based on the search results, here's what I found about Gemini CLI extensions marketplace registry:

## Extensions Gallery/Marketplace

The Extensions Gallery is the central marketplace for discovering all official Google-built and third-party extensions, available at https://geminicli.com/extensions/browse/. This gallery serves as the discoverability engine for the ecosystem.

## What Are Gemini CLI Extensions?

Gemini CLI extensions package prompts, MCP servers, custom commands, themes, hooks, sub-agents, and agent skills into a familiar and user-friendly format. Extensions are pre-packaged, easily installable integrations that connect Gemini CLI to external tools including everything from databases and design platforms to payment services.

## Registry Contents

A Gemini CLI Extensions page helps users discover a growing catalog of community, partner and Google-built extensions, ranked by popularity by GitHub stars. Extensions are available from Google, plus industry leaders like Dynatrace, Elastic, Figma, Harness, Postman, Shopify, Snyk and Stripe, and the broader open-source community.

## Important Notice

The extensions listed in the registry are sourced from public repositories and created by third-party developers. Google does not vet, endorse, or guarantee the functionality or security of these extensions. Users should carefully inspect any extension and its source code before installing.

## Search Results

I found comprehensive information about the nanobanana extension for Google Gemini CLI. Here's what I discovered:

### Overview
Nanobanana is a professional Gemini CLI extension for generating and manipulating images using the Nano Banana models.

### Key Features
The extension provides text-to-image generation, image editing with natural language instructions, image restoration for old or damaged photos, and smart file management with user-friendly filenames and automatic duplicate prevention.

### Installation
The extension can be installed using the gemini extensions install command: `gemini extensions install https://github.com/gemini-cli-extensions/nanobanana`, followed by restarting the Gemini CLI.

### Available Commands
The extension provides `/generate` for single or multiple image generation with style/variation options, and `/edit` for modifying existing images. The `/generate` command supports advanced options including --count for number of variations (1-8), --styles for comma-separated artistic styles, --variations for specific variation types, --format for output format, --seed for reproducible variations, and --preview for automatically opening generated images.

### Authentication
API Key setup requires setting the NANOBANANA_API_KEY environment variable with your Gemini API key. Users can get an API key from Google AI Studio.

### GitHub Repository
The official repository is available at **https://github.com/gemini-cli-extensions/nanobanana**

Here are the search results for the Gemini extensions nanobanana installation:

## Installation

To install the nanobanana extension, use the command: `gemini extensions install https://github.com/gemini-cli-extensions/nanobanana` and then restart the Gemini CLI.

## What is Nanobanana?

Nanobanana is a professional Gemini CLI extension for generating and manipulating images using the Nano Banana models. It provides text-to-image generation, image editing, image restoration, and smart file management features.

## Prerequisites

The MCP server requires Node.js 20+.

## API Key Setup

You'll need to set the NANOBANANA_API_KEY environment variable with your Gemini API key, which you can get from Google AI Studio.

## Available Commands

After installation, commands will be available including `/generate` for single or multiple image generation with style/variation options, and `/edit` for modifying existing images.

content.text:
Based on my search, here's what I found:

## Correct Installation URL for Nanobanana

The official GitHub repository is:
```
https://github.com/gemini-cli-extensions/nanobanana
```

Install it with:
```bash
gemini extensions install https://github.com/gemini-cli-extensions/nanobanana
```

Then restart the Gemini CLI.

## Official Extensions Marketplace

Yes, there is an official registry. Google maintains:
- **Extensions Gallery**: https://geminicli.com/extensions/ - The central marketplace for discovering all official and third-party extensions
- **Extensions Documentation**: https://geminicli.com/docs/extensions/

The gallery displays extensions ranked by GitHub stars and includes contributions from Google, industry partners (Dynatrace, Elastic, Figma, Shopify, Stripe, etc.), and the open-source community.

## Important Notes

- The extension requires **Node.js 20+**
- You'll need to set the `NANOBANANA_API_KEY` environment variable with your Gemini API key (obtainable from [Google AI Studio](https://aistudio.google.com))
- After installation, you'll have access to `/generate` (text-to-image) and `/edit` (image modification) commands

Sources:
- [GitHub - gemini-cli-extensions/nanobanana](https://github.com/gemini-cli-extensions/nanobanana)
- [Gemini CLI Extensions Documentation](https://geminicli.com/docs/extensions/)
- [Browse Extensions on Gemini CLI](https://geminicli.com/extensions/)
- [Getting Started with Gemini CLI Extensions](https://codelabs.developers.google.com/getting-started-gemini-cli-extensions)
